title: bandit: hardcoded SQL string construction in triage_repository
evidence: bandit B608 (MEDIUM/MEDIUM) flags 14 hardcoded SQL expressions built via string formatting in this file
risk: If any interpolated fragment carries user input not passed as a Data API parameter, SQL injection / tenant-isolation bypass is possible
recommendation: Verify every flagged statement passes user-controlled values as Data API named parameters; only static identifiers may be interpolated

title: bandit: hardcoded SQL string construction in repositories.py
evidence: bandit B608 (MEDIUM/MEDIUM) flags 11 hardcoded SQL expressions built via string formatting in this file
risk: User input interpolated into SQL rather than passed as a Data API parameter would allow injection
recommendation: Confirm all dynamic values use Data API parameters; restrict interpolation to whitelisted static identifiers/ORDER BY columns

title: bandit: hardcoded SQL string construction in label_enrichment repository
evidence: bandit B608 (MEDIUM/MEDIUM) flags 7 hardcoded SQL expressions in this file
risk: Injection if interpolated fragments include user/vendor-controlled input not parameterized
recommendation: Audit each statement; parameterize all dynamic values via the Data API

title: bandit: hardcoded SQL string construction in tags_repository
evidence: bandit B608 (MEDIUM/MEDIUM) flags 7 hardcoded SQL expressions in this file
risk: Injection / cross-tenant access if user-supplied tag data is interpolated into SQL
recommendation: Parameterize all dynamic values; verify user_id predicates are present

title: bandit: hardcoded SQL string construction in artist_enrichment repository
evidence: bandit B608 (MEDIUM/MEDIUM) flags 6 hardcoded SQL expressions in this file
risk: Injection if interpolated fragments include unparameterized input
recommendation: Audit and parameterize all dynamic values via the Data API

title: bandit: hardcoded SQL string construction in playlists_repository
evidence: bandit B608 (MEDIUM/MEDIUM) flags 5 hardcoded SQL expressions in this file
risk: Injection / IDOR if playlist ids or names are interpolated rather than parameterized
recommendation: Parameterize all dynamic values; verify user_id scoping on every query

title: bandit: hardcoded SQL string construction in label_enrichment auto_repository
evidence: bandit B608 (MEDIUM/MEDIUM) flags 3 hardcoded SQL expressions in this file
risk: Injection if interpolated fragments include unparameterized input
recommendation: Audit and parameterize all dynamic values via the Data API

title: bandit: hardcoded SQL string construction in artist_enrichment auto_repository
evidence: bandit B608 (MEDIUM/MEDIUM) flags 3 hardcoded SQL expressions in this file
risk: Injection if interpolated fragments include unparameterized input
recommendation: Audit and parameterize all dynamic values via the Data API

title: bandit: hardcoded SQL string construction in categories_repository
evidence: bandit B608 (MEDIUM/MEDIUM) flags 2 hardcoded SQL expressions in this file
risk: Injection if interpolated fragments include unparameterized input
recommendation: Parameterize all dynamic values via the Data API

title: bandit: urllib urlopen with audited scheme in vendor/oauth clients
evidence: bandit B310 (MEDIUM/HIGH) at spotify_client.py:411,449; beatport_client.py:113; auth/spotify_oauth.py:57; auth/ytmusic_oauth.py:74 — urllib.urlopen permits file:/ftp: schemes
risk: If any opened URL is influenced by external input, SSRF or local-file read could result; low risk if URLs are static vendor endpoints
recommendation: Confirm all URLs are constructed from static vendor bases; prefer requests/httpx or validate scheme is https

title: pnpm audit: react-router DoS via unbounded path expansion (__manifest)
evidence: pnpm audit reports a HIGH advisory: react-router vulnerable to DoS via unbounded path expansion in the __manifest endpoint
risk: Client-shipped dependency; a crafted request could exhaust resources depending on routing mode
recommendation: Bump react-router to the patched version per the advisory

title: pnpm audit: vitest UI server arbitrary file read/execute
evidence: pnpm audit reports a CRITICAL advisory: when the Vitest UI server is listening, an arbitrary file can be read and executed
risk: Dev/test-only dependency; only exploitable when the Vitest UI server runs and is reachable — not in the production runtime
recommendation: Bump vitest to the patched version; never expose the Vitest UI server on a routable interface

title: pnpm audit: ws memory-exhaustion DoS from tiny fragments
evidence: pnpm audit reports a HIGH advisory: ws memory exhaustion DoS from tiny fragments and data chunks
risk: Transitive dependency; production impact depends on whether ws serves runtime traffic (typically dev tooling only)
recommendation: Bump the dependency pulling in ws to a version requiring the patched ws

title: pnpm audit: form-data CRLF injection via unescaped field names
evidence: pnpm audit reports a HIGH advisory: CRLF injection in form-data via unescaped multipart field names and filenames
risk: Transitive build/test dependency; low runtime exposure unless form-data is used against attacker-controlled field names
recommendation: Bump the transitive form-data to the patched version

title: pnpm audit: vite server.fs.deny bypass on Windows alternate paths
evidence: pnpm audit reports a HIGH advisory: vite server.fs.deny bypass on Windows alternate data-stream paths
risk: Dev-server-only and Windows-only; not part of the deployed artifact
recommendation: Bump vite to the patched version

title: checkov: API Gateway routes without authorization_type
evidence: checkov CKV_AWS_309 fails on 4 routes: auth_login, auth_callback, auth_refresh, auth_logout (auth.tf:169-187) — no authorization_type set
risk: Routes without an authorizer are public; for the four auth endpoints this is intended, but the scan must be confirmed to cover ONLY those and no curation/collector route
recommendation: Confirm all 4 are the deliberately-public auth endpoints; ensure every non-auth route sets the JWT authorizer

title: checkov: RDS cluster lacks deletion protection
evidence: checkov CKV_AWS_139 fails: aws_rds_cluster.aurora has deletion_protection disabled
risk: An accidental terraform destroy or console deletion permanently destroys the canonical catalogue and all per-user data
recommendation: Set deletion_protection = true on the Aurora cluster (and confirm a final snapshot is configured)

title: checkov: Lambda functions without a Dead Letter Queue
evidence: checkov CKV_AWS_116 fails on 11 Lambda functions (collector, curation, auth_handler, auth_authorizer, workers, ...) — no on-failure DLQ destination
risk: Async invocation failures (timeouts, crashes) are silently dropped with no DLQ to inspect or replay — aligns with the known silent-failure class
recommendation: Configure on-failure destinations / DLQs for async Lambdas and alarm on DLQ depth

title: checkov: Lambda functions without reserved concurrency limit
evidence: checkov CKV_AWS_115 fails on 7 Lambda functions — no function-level concurrency limit
risk: A queue backlog or retry storm can fan out unbounded concurrent executions, multiplying Aurora wakeups and paid vendor calls
recommendation: Set reserved_concurrent_executions on worker/enrichment Lambdas to cap blast radius and spend

title: checkov: IAM policy allows '*' resource for restrictable actions
evidence: checkov CKV_AWS_356 fails: aws_iam_policy_document.collector_lambda grants actions on Resource '*'
risk: Over-broad permissions widen the blast radius if the collector Lambda is compromised
recommendation: Scope each action to specific resource ARNs where AWS supports resource-level permissions

title: checkov: IAM policy allows unconstrained write access
evidence: checkov CKV_AWS_111 fails: aws_iam_policy_document.collector_lambda allows write actions without resource constraints
risk: Compromise of the collector role permits broad writes beyond its intended scope
recommendation: Constrain write actions to specific ARNs / add conditions

title: checkov: Lambda environment variables not encrypted with CMK
evidence: checkov CKV_AWS_173 fails on 11 Lambda functions — env vars use the default key, not a customer-managed KMS key
risk: Env vars are encrypted at rest by AWS default key; CMK is defense-in-depth. Relevant only if any secret/token is stored in env vars
recommendation: Confirm no token/secret lives in Lambda env vars (use Secrets Manager); optionally enable a CMK

title: checkov: SQS queues not encrypted at rest
evidence: checkov CKV_AWS_27 fails on 12 SQS queues — no server-side encryption configured
risk: Queue payloads (ingest/enrichment messages) sit unencrypted at rest; low risk if no token/PII is in message bodies
recommendation: Enable SSE-SQS (sqs_managed_sse_enabled) on all queues; verify no token is ever placed in a message body

title: checkov: RDS cluster has no AWS Backup plan
evidence: checkov CKV2_AWS_8 fails: aws_rds_cluster.aurora is not covered by an AWS Backup plan
risk: Recovery depends solely on automated snapshots/PITR; verify retention window is adequate for MVP RPO
recommendation: Confirm backup_retention_period and PITR meet the recovery target; optionally add an AWS Backup plan

title: checkov: CloudWatch log groups retain logs under 1 year
evidence: checkov CKV_AWS_338 fails on 11 log groups — retention below 1 year (or unset)
risk: If retention is UNSET, logs never expire and storage cost grows unbounded; if intentionally short, that is a cost choice, not a defect
recommendation: Confirm every log group sets an explicit bounded retention_in_days (not unset)
